data.title: Markets Just Made History. 23 Stats That Prove It. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Matthew Bemer

March came in like a lion. And by the time a lamb came, it was too late.

Stocks surged on Tuesday, but it still wasn't enough to reverse a major pullback brought on by the Iran war. The Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq Composite both entered correction territory in March — a 10% drop from a recent high. The S&P 500 ended the month 3.8 percentage points away.

Don't forget the energy shock. With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, both U.S. and international crude prices soared to above $100 a barrel.

It has been a historically bad start to the year in many other ways — and not as bad as it could've been in some ways. Barron's has discussed what happened and how to prepare your portfolio, and if you want to read more, here are some useful links.

But perspective is everything, and something difficult to attain when following daily, hourly, or even minute-by-minute movements. So we compiled a list of telling monthly and quarterly statistics with Dow Jones Market Data below.

Stocks

  • The Dow fell 5.4% in March, its largest one-month percentage decline since September 2022.
  • The Dow also had its worst quarter since the third quarter of 2022.
  • The S&P 500 fell 5.1% in March, its largest one-month percentage decline since March 2025.
  • The S&P 500 also had its worst quarter since the second quarter of 2022.
  • The Nasdaq fell 4.8% in March, its largest one-month percentage decline since March 2025.
  • The Nasdaq also had its worst quarter since the first quarter of 2025.

Energy

  • U.S. oil prices, as measured by futures contracts for May delivery of West Texas Intermediate, rose 77% to $101.38 this quarter, the largest quarterly percentage gain since the second quarter of 2020.
  • They also recorded their biggest monthly percentage gain since May 2020.
  • International oil prices, as measured by futures contracts for May delivery of Brent crude, rose 95% to $118.35 this quarter, the largest quarterly percentage gain since the third quarter of 1990.
  • They also notched their biggest monthly percentage gain on record.
  • Wholesale gasoline prices, measured by futures contracts for April delivery of RBOB gasoline, rose 60% in March, the biggest monthly gain on record.
  • The national U.S. average price for one gallon of regular unleaded gasoline ended the month at $4.02, the highest level since August 2022.
  • Natural-gas prices in Europe, as measured by Dutch TTF futures, climbed 59% in March, the biggest monthly jump since June 2022.
  • They're up 80% this quarter, the biggest quarterly jump since the third quarter of 2021.

Precious Metals

  • Front-month gold futures had their largest monthly percentage drop since June 2013.
  • Silver futures fell the most in March since September 2011 in percentage terms.

Bonds

  • The 2-year Treasury yield rose to 3.799%, snapping a four-quarter streak of falling yields.
  • The 10-year Treasury yield rose for the second straight quarter to 4.310%.
  • The 30-year Treasury yield rose for the second straight quarter to 4.890%.

Currencies

  • The WSJ Dollar Index saw its largest quarterly percentage gain since the third quarter of 2025.
  • The index is also up for the third straight quarter.
  • The euro lost 1.7% vs. the U.S. dollar, the largest one-quarter percentage decline since the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Bitcoin snapped a five-month losing streak.

Write to editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AT&T signs deal worth $2 billion to upgrade emergency cellular network
data.description:
AT&T has reached a deal to invest about $1 billion to improve the Commerce Department's FirstNet and deliver $1 billion in cost savings for the program via reduced rates, a U.S government agency said on Tuesday.

The telecommunications company was awarded the 25-year contract to build the federal emergency cellular network FirstNet in 2017, years after a federal commission recommended setting up such a system following the 9/11 attacks.

The system, which helps first responders such as medical personnel, firefighters and police officers communicate vital information on a single network, is used by 31,000 U.S. agencies.

The agreement was made possible through U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order in early 2025, asking federal agencies to review all contracts, the National Telecommunications and Information Administration said in a statement.

"This agreement-in-principle... reflects AT&T's ongoing dedication to our public-private partnership," AT&T's President of Public Sector Wes Anderson said.

data.title: Arafura Rare Earths Agrees Binding Equity Deals With Germany, Australia
data.description:
By Rhiannon Hoyle

Arafura Rare Earths said it has signed equity deals with Germany's state-owned development bank and Australia's export credit agency as it works to finalize its funding for the Nolans rare earth project in Australia's Northern Territory.

Arafura said Wednesday that the binding agreements include roughly 84 million Australian dollars (US$58.0) from Germany's Kreditanstalt fur Wiederaufbau, on behalf of the German Raw Materials Fund. It has also signed a deal valued at roughly A$146 million with Export Finance Australia.

The company had previously signed nonbinding agreements with both agencies.

"The cornerstone equity commitments supplement the company's debt financing arrangements announced in July 2024, positioning Arafura closer to achieving financial close and a final investment decision for the Nolans project," the company said.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: Investor Artisan Partners backs Unilever's plan to sell food unit
data.description:
Key points:
  • Artisan Partners calls Unilever-McCormick deal logical, tax-efficient, and good value
  • Unilever shares fall 7% after deal announcement, other investors express concerns
  • Artisan Partners says Unilever can now focus on faster-growing personal and home care brands

By Richa Naidu

Artisan Partners, which has a history of driving strategic change at consumer companies, welcomed Unilever's decision on Tuesday to merge its food business with McCormick , saying the Dove soap maker should now be able to "more effectively manage" its core personal care and home brands.

"The company will now more logically separate its interests in the food business and the personal care business," David Samra, managing director of Artisan Partners and founding partner of the International Value Group, told Reuters. He said the deal was also tax-efficient and had given shareholders "an attractive sale price."

The merger will create a company worth around $65 billion in the second-largest food transaction in history after Kraft and Heinz's deal in 2015.

Unilever's food unit is a high-margin business, but sales growth has lagged the company's personal goods and beauty businesses and weighed on its ambition to increase overall group sales by 4%-6% in the near term.

Investor pressure on Unilever to shed food brands increased after it was revealed in 2022 that billionaire activist-shareholder Nelson Peltz had built a stake in the company. Peltz has been linked to the departure of former CEOs Alan Jope and Hein Schumacher, with Fernando Fernandez, Unilever's former finance chief and a veteran beauty and wellbeing executive, promoted to CEO to focus on streamlining the company's portfolio.

Artisan has a $1.6 billion stake in Unilever, or 1.22% of the company's shares. It is the British company's ninth-largest investor, according to data from LSEG's Workspace. By comparison, Peltz, a Unilever board member, owns a $1.73 billion stake in the company as its seventh-biggest investor.

"The remaining Unilever businesses operate in faster-growing and highly profitable categories and geographic markets," Samra said, adding: "On a standalone basis, these businesses should be able to command a higher earnings multiple."

Unilever did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Other investors on Tuesday found the reality of the deal Unilever and McCormick struck hard to digest, perturbed by the transaction's structure, its long timeline to closing and the potential for antitrust scrutiny.

Shares in ‌Unilever, owner of Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes, fell by 7% after the deal was announced, wiping $7 billion from its market value. McCormick, owner of Cholula hot sauce, also took a hit as its shares slid by about 5%.

Artisan has in recent years advocated for change at several major global companies, from German pharmaceutical giant Bayer to chocolate maker Barry Callebaut. In early 2021, Artisan in an open letter called for change at Danone , saying it had built a stake of more than 3% in the French food giant. About a month later, Danone's then-CEO and chairman, Emmanuel Faber, was ousted and its board overhauled.

More recently, Artisan became the second-largest shareholder in Barry Callebaut with a roughly 10% stake.

data.title: Patriot National Bancorp 10-K: Total interest $47.8M, EPS $(0.17)
data.description:
Patriot National Bancorp reported total interest and dividend income of $47.8 million and a net loss of $12.7 million, or $(0.17) per share, reflecting lower loan balances and a shift toward lower-yielding cash equivalents that pressured net interest income. Net interest income declined to $19.1 million with a net interest margin of 2.04%, while improvements in credit trends reduced the provision for credit losses to $1.5 million. Non‑interest income grew to $10.5 million but was insufficient to offset higher operating expenses, leaving results improved versus the prior-year loss but still negative for the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest and dividend income: $47.8 million — decline vs. prior year due to lower average loan balances and a shift to lower-yielding cash equivalents.
  • Net interest income: $19.1 million — decreased from $20.1 million; net interest margin 2.04% (prior 2.14%).
  • Provision for credit losses: $1.5 million — substantially lower than $12.5 million in 2024, reflecting improved credit trends.
  • Non‑interest income: $10.5 million — up from $8.4 million, driven by growth in digital payments fees.
  • Non‑interest expense: $40.8 million — increased from $32.1 million due to higher compensation and professional fees.
  • Net (loss) income: $(12.7) million — improved from $(39.9) million in the prior year.
  • Basic and diluted (loss) per share: $(0.17), improved versus prior-year $(10.03) per share.

Business Highlights

  • Liquidity and funding flexibility: Increased cash and unpledged securities materially boosted on-hand liquidity and operational funding flexibility.
  • Balance sheet repositioning: Management reduced loan originations and increased investment securities to lower portfolio risk.
  • Core channels — digital payments: Digital payments remained a key channel, with fee income rising from larger program managers and continued origination and sale of credit-card receivables.
  • Credit remediation and portfolio quality: Lower net charge-offs and reduced nonperforming assets after resolution of troubled loans improved credit stability.
  • Operational reset and staffing: Operating costs increased due to management transitions, remediation efforts, and staffing realignment to support strategic repositioning.

Original SEC Filing: PATRIOT NATIONAL BANCORP INC [ PNBK ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Digi Power X 10-K: $34.19M Revenue, $(0.64) EPS
data.description:
Digi Power X reported $34.19 million in revenue for the year and a net loss of $28.36 million, driven by revaluation, foreign exchange and warrant losses that widened the loss from the prior year; basic and diluted loss per share was $(0.64). Revenue declined versus the prior year and the company recorded a gross loss and a larger operating loss as share‑based compensation and digital currency revaluations increased operating expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $34.19 million (decreased from $37.00 million prior year)
  • Gross loss (Gross Profit): $(3.22) million
  • Operating (loss): $(23.09) million
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(28.36) million
  • Net income per share: $(0.64) basic and diluted (weighted average shares 44.46 million)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Transitioned from bitcoin mining toward colocation and energy sales, reducing mined bitcoin revenue while increasing colocation and energy income.
  • Capacity expansion: Consolidated capacity grew to approximately 90 MW across three sites, with total available capacity across assets of 196.7 MW and board approval for an additional 60 MW load in New York.
  • AI/HPC strategic pivot: Launched a US Data Centers subsidiary and introduced ARMS modular AI pods; first ARMS pod and an NVIDIA B200 GPU cluster are on track for deployment in Q2 2026.
  • Commercial partnerships and deployments: Executed colocation and profit‑share agreements covering roughly 11,000 S21 miners (~44 MW) and launched NeoCloudz retail GPU compute in January 2026.
  • Sustainability and grid support: Targeting 100% carbon neutrality by 2026, leveraging an approximately 89% zero‑carbon grid mix in New York and offering demand‑response and load flexibility services.

Original SEC Filing: Digi Power X Inc. [ DGXX ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics 2025 10-K: $0.0M Revenue, $(1.11) EPS
data.description:
Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics reported no revenue for the period ending December 31, 2025, and posted a net loss of $10.307 million, equivalent to a basic and diluted net loss per share of $(1.11). The company recorded an operating loss of $9.953 million driven by research and development and general and administrative expenses. Gross profit is zero given the absence of reported revenue.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.0 million (no revenues from operations for the period).
  • Gross Profit: $0.0 million (no revenue reported).
  • Operating Income: $(9.953) million (operating loss).
  • Net Income: $(10.307) million (net loss for year ended Dec 31, 2025).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(1.11) basic and diluted net loss per share.

Business Highlights

  • Regulatory Milestone: Received a Special Protocol Assessment (SPA) from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for the NurOwn® Phase 3b ALS trial, confirming the trial design and planned analyses.
  • Clinical Focus & Pipeline: Advancing NurOwn for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and exploring applications in progressive multiple sclerosis (PMS), Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and other central nervous system indications using its proprietary mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) platform.
  • Operational Activity: Continued research and development and clinical operations during 2025, with reduced R&D spending and streamlined general and administrative functions compared to prior periods.
  • Funding & Go‑forward Plan: Engaged multiple financing routes, including at-the-market (ATM) offerings, private placements and convertible notes, to support the Phase 3b trial and potential commercialization activities.

Original SEC Filing: BRAINSTORM CELL THERAPEUTICS INC. [ BCLI ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Aptose Biosciences 10-K: No Revenue, Net Loss $(25.468)M, EPS $(10.41)
data.description:
Aptose Biosciences reported no revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025 and posted a net loss of $25.468 million, equivalent to net loss per common share of $10.41. The company recorded operating losses driven by research and development and general and administrative expenses. The filing outlines clinical and operational progress on its lead program while detailing corporate and manufacturing actions supporting ongoing trials.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $— (no revenue reported for the years ended Dec 31, 2025 and 2024)
  • Operating Income: $— (not presented; R&D $11.341M and G&A $13.382M resulted in an operating loss)
  • Net Income: $(25.468) million (net loss and comprehensive loss for the year ended Dec 31, 2025)
  • Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: $(10.41) (for the year ended Dec 31, 2025)

Business Highlights

  • Clinical Progress: Advanced tuspetinib through Phase 1/2 into a frontline combination trial (TUS+VEN+AZA, “TUSCANY”), reporting multiple complete remissions and high rates of MRD‑negativity across diverse AML mutations.
  • Safety & Differentiation: Tuspetinib demonstrated a favorable safety profile with no dose‑limiting toxicities, no QTc signal, and no differentiation syndrome observed, supporting dose escalation to 160 mg and enabling outpatient oral dosing.
  • Partnerships & Trials: Entered a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement (CRADA) with the National Cancer Institute to support MyeloMATCH trials and completed an international APTIVATE expansion to broaden clinical sites and investigator engagement.
  • Manufacturing & Supply: Secured existing drug product inventory and a supply agreement with Hanmi; additional API and drug product batches were produced to support ongoing clinical trials.
  • Corporate Actions & Market Access: Shareholders approved an arrangement with Hanmi to acquire the company at C$2.41 per share, with subsequent delisting from the TSX pending closing of the arrangement.

Original SEC Filing: Aptose Biosciences Inc. [ APTOF ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: 20/20 Biolabs 10-K: Revenues $2.05M, Net Loss $(3.74)M
data.description:
20/20 Biolabs reported fiscal-year revenue of $2.045 million, a 16.7% increase versus the prior year, driven by higher OneTest and CLIAx sales, and recorded a net loss of $(3.739) million (net loss per share not disclosed). Gross profit improved to $0.605 million with a 29.56% margin, while the company reported an operating loss of $(3.331) million as operating expenses remained elevated relative to revenues.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2.045 million, up 16.7% year‑over‑year.
  • Gross Profit: $0.605 million; gross margin 29.56% (vs. 20.56% prior year).
  • Operating Loss: $(3.331) million; operating expenses were 192.43% of revenues.
  • Net Loss: $(3.739) million, improved 32.7% versus prior year.
  • Net Loss Per Share: Not stated in the provided filing excerpts.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Drivers: Growth was driven by higher OneTest sales and a shift to a premium test mix, including price increases that improved average revenue per unit.
  • Product Mix: Increased sales of Premium OneTest (about 35% more sold) contributed to improved product margins.
  • New Product Launch: Launched OneTest for Longevity in February 2026.
  • Market Expansion: Began multi-cancer screenings for veterans, indicating expansion of clinical service offerings.
  • Operational Capacity: All testing is performed in the company’s CLIA laboratory in Gaithersburg; the company operates a CLIAx shared lab model for international startups.
  • Cost and Efficiency Initiatives: Gross margin improvement and reductions in SG&A and R&D were achieved through in‑house marketing and prioritization of R&D efforts.

Original SEC Filing: 20/20 Biolabs, Inc. [ AIDX ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: West African Resources's Costs Higher Than Bull Expected — Market Talk
data.description: West African Resources faces higher costs this year than Euroz Hartleys initially thought. West African Resources has forecast all-in sustaining costs of up to US$1,900/oz. That represents a 31% increase on its costs in 2025. Analyst Mike Millikan highlights a key cost variable at the Kiaka mine in Burkina Faso. The mine relies on diesel back‑up generation in place of available grid power. Under current conditions, this is estimated to add US$300/oz to AISC, driving the higher-than-expected costs. "West African Resources plans to purchase heavy fuel oil back-up power generators for Kiaka, with targeted commissioning of early 2027," Euroz Hartleys says. "Reflecting this, we revise our AISC forecast from US$1,549/oz to US$1,786/oz AISC for 2026." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Binah Capital Group 10-K: $187.1M Revenue, Net Income $2.3M
data.description:
Binah Capital Group reported fiscal 2025 total revenue of $187.1 million, up 10.8% from $168.9 million a year earlier, and swung to a net profit of $2.3 million versus a net loss of $4.6 million in 2024. Gross profit increased to $37.8 million and operating income rose to $2.6 million, reflecting improved profitability driven by higher commissions and advisory fees. Management notes continued sensitivity to equity markets and interest-rate dynamics that could influence future asset levels and results.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $187.1 million, increased 10.8% vs. 2024 ($168.9M)
  • Gross profit: $37.8 million, up ~12% vs. 2024 ($33.7M)
  • Operating income: $2.6 million, improvement from loss before tax in 2024 (-$3.1M)
  • Net income: $2.3 million, vs. net loss of ($4.6 million) in 2024
  • Net income per share: Not provided in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8 and therefore omitted

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Higher commissions and advisory fees drove the year-over-year revenue increase and helped improve operational profitability.
  • Channel mix shift: Brokerage assets grew to $27.0 billion while advisory assets climbed to $2.9 billion, with both trailing and sales-based commissions rising.
  • Advisor economics: Advisor payout rate remained around 75.4%, while sales-based commissions increased roughly 13%, indicating higher transactional activity and sustained advisor productivity.
  • Operational scaling: Increased technology and public-company personnel costs supported platform expansion and compliance efforts during the year.
  • Outlook and sensitivities: Management highlights that asset growth was aided by market gains and that future performance remains sensitive to equity market moves and interest-rate dynamics, which may affect asset levels and fee-related revenue.

Original SEC Filing: Binah Capital Group, Inc. [ BCG ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Arena REIT Ticks Defensive Boxes in Uncertain Times — Market Talk
data.description: Australian property companies with conservative balance sheets, well-positioned hedge books and inflation-protected income streams should be rewarded in the current environment, says Jefferies. Arena REIT ticks all 3 of these boxes, analyst Andrew Dodds says. "Gearing of 23% is one of the lowest across the A-REIT sector and hedging of 93% (at a low fixed rate of 2.7%) limits downside risk to future earnings growth," Jefferies says. Also, Arena REIT has 88% of income linked to the Consumer Price Index, which is set to benefit in a higher inflation environment. Jefferies retains a buy call and A$3.86/share price target on Arena REIT, which ended Tuesday at A$3.27. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Amrize Names Oran CFO; Johnston to Step Down
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Amrize named Baris Oran chief financial officer, effective April 1.

Oran will succeed Ian Johnston, who is stepping down from the role. Johnston will remain with Amrize as a senior adviser to support the transition, the building solutions company said.

Oran most recently served as CFO of GXO Logistics following that company's spinoff from XPO. He has also served as CFO of the Sabanci Group and Kordsa, and has held leadership roles at PwC, Ernst & Young, and Sara Lee Corp.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com
